name: Onboarding Pulse - Backstory Starter
description: Monitors newly closed deals during their first 90 days to detect accounts going dark before they become a retention problem. The workflow identifies recently closed-won accounts, checks Backstory engagement data for post-sale activity (meetings booked, emails exchanged, contacts engaged), and flags accounts with below-threshold engagement. An AI agent assesses each flagged account and recommends specific re-engagement actions. Alerts are sent to the CSM and sales handoff team via Messaging.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch closed-won accounts from the last 90 days with close date, owners, and onboarding status. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch closed-won accounts from the last 90 days with close date, owners, and onboarding status.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw new customers in onboarding responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze Onboarding Pulse

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Onboarding Pulse workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Monitors newly closed deals during their first 90 days to detect accounts going dark before they become a retention problem. The workflow identifies recently closed-won accounts, checks Backstory engagement data for post-sale activity (meetings booked, emails exchanged, contacts engaged), and flags accounts with below-threshold engagement. An AI agent assesses each flagged account and recommends specific re-engagement actions. Alerts are sent to the CSM and sales handoff team via Messaging.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the onboarding pulse use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
👶 **Onboarding Pulse** — 2 new customers need attention

🔴 **GOING DARK — Immediate Re-engagement Needed:**
- **Dunder Mifflin** | ===$125,000=== | Closed Won: Feb 3 | Day 34 of 90
- Post-sale engagement: **0 meetings, 2 emails** (benchmark: 4 meetings, 12 emails by Day 34)
- Champion Michael Scott hasn't responded to last 3 CSM emails
- No kickoff meeting scheduled — implementation hasn't started
- 👉 @emily.ross: Escalate to sales handoff team. @sarah.chen (AE) should call champion directly — leverage closing relationship

🟡 **AT RISK — Below Engagement Threshold:**

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Onboarding Pulse outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
